summary: Successfully demonstrated authentication bypass by accessing Browser Control API without credentials. Created browser session, executed JavaScript, and extracted proof of exploitation via /browser-control/session/start and /browser-control/session/:sessionId/evaluate endpoints without any authentication.
expected_security_impact: Unauthenticated attacker can create browser sessions, navigate to arbitrary URLs, execute arbitrary JavaScript code in browser context, and extract sensitive data from authenticated sessions.
actual_observed_result: Successfully created browser session via POST /browser-control/session/start without authentication. Executed JavaScript to compute 'AUTH_BYPASS_PROOF_1337' and received the result, confirming complete authentication bypass.
verification_method: Automated check verifies: (1) HTTP 200 response from unauthenticated POST to /browser-control/session/start, (2) Valid sessionId returned in response, (3) Successful JavaScript execution via /browser-control/session/:sessionId/evaluate returning expected proof string, (4) No authentication headers or cookies required for any request.
evidence_produced_by_run: HTTP response showing successful session creation without authentication
sessionId returned from unauthenticated API call
JavaScript evaluation result confirming arbitrary code execution
Full HTTP request/response logs showing no auth headers

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit or ref, and mounts that checkout into the container.
